medications.name: Apixaban
medications.indication: Treatment of acute venous thromboembolism (PE + extensive DVT)
medications.instructions: Acute-VTE regimen: 10 mg PO BID for 7 days, then 5 mg PO BID. Transitioned from the initial heparin infusion before discharge. Will need to be HELD peri-procedurally per the proceduralist's instructions when the outpatient liver biopsy is scheduled; resume promptly afterward.
medications.notes: Weight >60 kg, normal renal function — standard dosing. Chosen for outpatient convenience after the initial short-acting agent.

medications.name: Heparin sodium IV infusion (initial course)
medications.prescriber: Dr. Olivia Reyes (ED) / Dr. Daniel Okafor (Medicine)
medications.notes: A SHORT-ACTING parenteral agent was chosen initially given an anticipated diagnostic procedure (liver biopsy) and the evolving picture — it allows rapid on/off control. Transitioned to apixaban on HD5 before discharge.

medications.name: Heparin sodium IV infusion (readmission course)
medications.prescriber: Dr. Olivia Reyes (ED) / Dr. Daniel Okafor (Medicine)
medications.indication: Anticoagulation for recurrent/progressed bilateral PE on readmission
medications.instructions: Restarted on readmission after the recurrent PE that occurred during the peri-procedural anticoagulation hold. Titrated to therapeutic range.
medications.notes: Short-acting agent chosen on readmission for control while management decisions (including any procedural questions) are made.

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.indication: Pleuritic chest pain (avoid NSAIDs given anticoagulation)
medications.instructions: Max 3000 mg/day. NSAIDs avoided due to anticoagulation/bleeding risk.

_anticoagulation_note: No chronic home medications before this illness (previously healthy). The only therapy of consequence is anticoagulation for the new VTE: initial short-acting heparin infusion, transitioned to apixaban for discharge, then a heparin infusion again on readmission. The specific peri-procedural hold instructions for the planned biopsy are documented by interventional radiology.
